intro: Cynoglossum officinale, also known as Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, belonging to the Boraginaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antiviral, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum officinale include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Cynoglossum officinale is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, often used to treat coughs, bronchitis, and skin irritations, as well as to support the immune system.
